text: Nimbus Notes is a note-taking application for desktop and mobile. It organizes notes into notebooks, supports tags, and syncs across devices through a Nimbus account. The free plan includes unlimited notes on up to two devices.

Nimbus Notes offers three plans. The Free plan costs nothing and syncs two devices. The Plus plan costs 4 dollars per month and syncs unlimited devices with 10 gigabytes of monthly uploads. The Pro plan costs 8 dollars per month and adds version history and 50 gigabytes of monthly uploads.

Sync runs automatically every five minutes when the app is open and online. You can force a sync by pressing the refresh icon in the sidebar. If a sync conflict occurs, Nimbus keeps both versions and marks the note with a conflict badge.

Notes are cached locally so you can read and edit them offline. Edits made offline are queued and uploaded the next time the device connects. The Free plan caches only the 100 most recently opened notes for offline use.
Tags help you find notes across notebooks. Add a tag by typing a hash symbol followed by the tag name anywhere in a note. The tag panel on the left lists every tag and the number of notes using it.
Search matches note titles, body text, and tags. Use quotation marks for an exact phrase and the word OR between terms to broaden a search. Search results are ranked by recency and relevance.
You can export a single note or a whole notebook. Supported export formats are PDF, Markdown, and HTML. Exporting a notebook produces a ZIP archive containing one file per note.
Nimbus can import notes from Markdown files and from Evernote ENEX exports. During import you can choose a destination notebook and whether to preserve original timestamps.

Each note can hold attachments such as images, PDFs, and audio recordings. The maximum size of a single attachment is 25 megabytes on Plus and 100 megabytes on Pro. Attachments count toward your monthly upload allowance.
Share a note by generating a share link. A share link can be set to view-only or edit. View-only links can be opened by anyone, while edit links require the recipient to sign in to a Nimbus account.

Add a reminder to a note to receive a notification at a chosen time. Reminders appear in the Reminders view and can repeat daily, weekly, or monthly. Completing a reminder removes its notification but keeps the note.

Templates let you start a note from a predefined layout. Nimbus ships with templates for meeting notes, daily journals, and project plans. You can save any note as a custom template from the note menu.
Nimbus encrypts data in transit using TLS and at rest on its servers. You can enable two-factor authentication in account settings. Individual notebooks can be locked with a separate passcode on Pro.

Deleted notes move to Trash, where they remain for 30 days before permanent removal. You can restore a note from Trash to its original notebook. Emptying Trash deletes its contents immediately and cannot be undone.
Keyboard shortcuts speed up common actions. Press Control N to create a note, Control F to search, and Control Shift T to open the tag panel. On macOS use the Command key instead of Control.
Nimbus runs on Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS. There is also a web client that works in any modern browser. A single account can be signed in on as many devices as the plan allows.

If notes are not syncing, check that the device is online and signed in, then force a sync from the sidebar. If the app is slow, clearing the local cache from settings often helps. Contact support if a note appears corrupted.

Create a Nimbus account with an email address and password. You can change your email in account settings, which sends a confirmation link to the new address. Deleting your account permanently removes all notes after a 14-day grace period.
